title: thornbera grayi
intro: Thornbera grayi, also known as the Gray's thornapple, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the Solanaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Thornbera grayi include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing and skin health. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and roots are prepared as teas, infusions, or decoctions, and sometimes also used as a poultice or applied topically to affected areas.
intro_study: According to "Journal of complementary & integrative medicine", thornbera grayi, which is also known as Phragmenthera capitata, may be beneficial in fighting against melanoma due to its high antioxidant activity, high phenolic content, and its ability to promote apoptosis-induced cell death in cancer cells.
